content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. This sequence is circular ‚Äï for each valid iii, the element Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1‚Äã follows after AiA_iAi‚Äã, and the element A1A_1A1‚Äã follows after ANA_NAN‚Äã.

You may insert any positive integers at any positions you choose in this sequence; let's denote the resulting sequence by BBB. This sequence is also circular. For each pair of its elements BsB_sBs‚Äã and BfB_fBf‚Äã, let's denote the (non-circular) sequence created by starting at BsB_sBs‚Äã and moving from each element to the one that follows after it, until we reach BfB_fBf‚Äã, by B(s,f)B(s, f)B(s,f). This sequence includes the elements BsB_sBs‚Äã and BfB_fBf‚Äã.

For each KKK from 222 to NNN inclusive, find the smallest possible number of elements that need to be inserted into AAA to form a sequence BBB for which there is no subsequence B(p,q)B(p, q)B(p,q) such that:


The size of B(p,q)B(p, q)B(p,q) is at least KKK.
There is no pair of consecutive elements in B(p,q)B(p, q)B(p,q) such that their GCD is equal to 111.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚ãÖ106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 space-separated integers. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àí11 \le i \le N-11‚â§i‚â§N‚àí1), the iii-th of these integers should be the smallest number of inserted elements in a valid sequence BBB for K=i+1K = i+1K=i+1.
